---
title: "Clocks on the Web"
subtitle: "Preliminary Data Results"
date: "2026-03-26"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    scrollable: true
    smaller: true
---

## I did a zmap!

- Ran a `zmap` ICMP Timestamp scan from an allowed VPS*
- Scanned for hosts that respond to ICMP Timestamp requests
- And 3% of hosts do! (10% support regular ICMP echo)

\* My scan only includes 30% of the internet. The server got disconnected part way through :(

## Results

## Enrichment

For each responding host, I'm adding:

- **Reverse DNS** hostname
- **ICMP ping** RTT (ms)
- **GeoIP** (MaxMind): geo data like city, lat long
- **HTTP probe**: port 80 open? `Date:` header present?
- **Clock offset** (ICMP Timestamp hosts only): computed from timestamp fields

## Enriched Data

```
batch_num,ip,hostname,rtt_ms,is_http,had_date,country,city,latitude,longitude,daddr,otime,rtime,ttime,clock_offset_ms
0,81.245.55.131,131.55-245-81.adsl-dyn.isp.belgacom.be,18.0,false,false,BE,Brussels,50.8274,4.348,157.90.252.35,78205546,1401094144,1401094144,1322888589
...
```

```
batch_num,ip,hostname,rtt_ms,is_http,had_date,country,city,latitude,longitude,daddr,otime,rtime,ttime,clock_offset_ms
0,87.156.20.214,p579c14d6.dip0.t-ipconnect.de,135.0,false,false,DE,Aschaffenburg,49.9618,9.1634,,,,,
...
```

## Results: Clock Sync Map

<iframe src="plots/clock_sync_map.html" width="100%" height="600" style="border:none;"></iframe>

## Results: ICMP vs HTTP Clock Offset

<iframe src="plots/icmp_vs_http.html" width="100%" height="600" style="border:none;"></iframe>

## Next Steps

- Full internet zmap scan
- Explore the data further -- anything else I could enrich with? AS?
- Fingerprinting. OS?
- Do scans from more places?
- Stats on ping vs clockdiff
